hook: Luca cannot open the locked box, so he goes to the library to find out who the Cole family were — but while he is gone, someone enters his flat and takes the box.
goal: Luca wants to identify the Cole family by searching old records at the public library.

knowledge: The box was hidden under the Cole House floorboard by a man and a frightened woman on the tape
The name COLE is scratched on the box
Ray called the same morning Luca found the box and asked about value
Luca lied to Ray and said he found nothing
suspicions: Ray knows more about the box than he has said
Ray's call was not a coincidence

continuity_rules: Must open after Luca is already home with the box — do not re-show the walk back from Cole House
Must not repeat the detail of Ray's phone call as a new discovery — Luca already knows this
Must not contradict: Luca has the box, the camera, and the Cole House key at the start of this episode
key_events: Event 1 — Luca tries to force the box open with a kitchen knife; the metal does not give. THEREFORE he decides he must learn who the Cole family were before he can go further, and he leaves for the public library, setting the box on the table.

Event 2 — At the library Luca asks for old town newspapers. He finds a short article from July 1974: the Cole family left Harden without explanation; their house passed to a new owner shortly after. A property record from the same period carries a single name as witness to the sale: E. Ward. Luca writes both facts on a piece of paper.
Event 3 — Luca leaves the library and walks back to his flat. BUT when he reaches his door, it is slightly open — he did not leave it that way.
Event 4 — Luca steps inside. The table is bare. The drawer beside the bed is pulled open. The metal box is gone. Someone came in while he was at the library and took it.
major_reveal: The Cole family left Harden suddenly in July 1974 and never returned — their departure was unexplained and the house was quickly transferred to a new owner.
threads_advanced: thread_001 — the box is now stolen, raising the stakes of its contents
thread_002 — the theft suggests Ray or someone connected to him acted quickly to recover the box
thread_003 — the Cole family's 1974 departure is now confirmed as real and abrupt
thread_004 — the theft strongly implies Ray knew Luca had the box despite Luca's lie
new_thread_created: thread_005 — Who is E. Ward, whose name appears in the 1974 property record, and what was their connection to the Cole family's sudden departure?
tone_guardrails: Keep the theft quiet and physical — no violence, no confrontation, just an open door and an empty table
Only one major revelation: the 1974 departure; the theft is the reversal, not a second revelation
Do not have Luca immediately accuse Ray by name — suspicion only, no confirmation
forbidden_moves: Do not re-explain Ray's suspicious phone call as if it is new information — Luca already processed this
Do not reveal what is inside the box
Do not confirm that Ray stole the box — suspicion only
Do not introduce any character beyond a nameless librarian as a background helper
ending_hook: Luca stands in his empty flat staring at the bare table — the box is gone, and the only person who knew he had it was Ray Brennan.
